task_completion.description: A hierarchical diagram is created in draw.io starting with a 'Home' root node.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io interface is open, showing a diagram with a 'Home' node at the top or center, with lines connecting to child nodes.

task_completion.description: The diagram includes the correct top-level categories connected directly to 'Home'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Nodes labeled 'Men', 'Women', 'Gear', and 'Collections' are connected directly as children to the 'Home' node.

task_completion.description: The 'Outerwear' category is correctly placed and colored orange.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A node labeled 'Outerwear' is connected under 'Men' -> 'Tops'. The node has an orange fill color.

task_completion.description: The 'Yoga' category is correctly placed and colored green.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A node labeled 'Yoga' is connected under 'Gear'. The node has a green fill color.

task_completion.description: The 'Collections' and 'Eco-Friendly' categories are correctly placed and colored green.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Collections' node (connected to Home) and the 'Eco-Friendly' node (connected to Collections) both have a green fill color.

task_completion.description: The 'Tanks' category is excluded from the diagram.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Under the 'Women' -> 'Tops' hierarchy, there is no node labeled 'Tanks'. It should not appear anywhere in the diagram.

task_completion.description: The diagram is saved as a .drawio file and exported as a .png file to the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Save/Export dialogs showing the filenames 'luma_sitemap.drawio' and 'luma_sitemap.png' being saved to the Desktop directory, or the files appearing on the Desktop in a file manager or terminal.

integrity.description: The agent used the draw.io Desktop GUI to create the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the draw.io application interface with the diagram being actively edited, rather than just a script generating the XML file.

integrity.description: The agent opened and read the migration specs file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text file 'luma_migration_specs.txt' is seen open in a text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: The agent constructed the diagram manually rather than copying a pre-made file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen constructing the diagram step-by-step, adding nodes, changing colors, and typing labels in draw.io.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The diagram must be a hierarchy starting with 'Home'. Top-level categories are Men, Women, Gear, and Collections. Specific structural changes to verify: 1) 'Outerwear' is under Men > Tops and must be colored orange. 2) 'Yoga' is under Gear and must be colored green. 3) 'Collections' (top-level) and 'Eco-Friendly' (under Collections) must both be colored green. 4) 'Tanks' must NOT appear anywhere (specifically not under Women > Tops). Standard nodes should be white. The final files must be luma_sitemap.drawio and luma_sitemap.png on the Desktop.
